Platform & Data.narrative: This family ensures your team can access inventory data anytime, anywhere, and keeps customer and supplier information secure and compliant with Singapore's PDPA rules. Your warehouse staff can check stock on their phones, your finance team can export data to accounting software, and you maintain a complete audit trail of who changed what. For a Singapore SME, this is non-negotiable: you need mobile access for fast-moving operations, PDPA compliance to avoid regulatory fines, and seamless data flow to your accountant.

Inventory Core.narrative: This family gives you a single source of truth for every item you stock — from SKU codes and quantities on hand to the financial value of your inventory. Real-time stock visibility prevents overselling and stockouts, barcode scanning cuts picking errors, and lot tracking protects you if a batch needs recall. For a Singapore SME managing multiple SKUs or locations, this is the backbone: you avoid tying up cash in dead stock, fulfil orders faster, and stay compliant if a customer asks 'which batch did I receive?'

Procurement.narrative: This family streamlines how you order from suppliers — from raising a purchase order to tracking delivery and managing payment terms. A central supplier register lets you compare lead times and costs, and PO tracking ensures you know when goods are arriving. For a Singapore SME, this cuts procurement cycle time, reduces duplicate orders, and gives you leverage to negotiate better terms with vendors.

Warehouse & Logistics.narrative: This family orchestrates the physical movement of goods — receiving inbound stock, moving it between locations, picking and packing customer orders, and handling returns. Multi-location support lets you operate a main warehouse and a satellite store; dropship capability lets you fulfil orders without holding inventory; and assembly management supports light manufacturing or kitting. For a Singapore SME, this is critical: you reduce picking errors and labour cost, speed up order fulfilment, and can scale to multiple locations without chaos.

Planning & Compliance.narrative: This family automates replenishment decisions, predicts demand to avoid stockouts, calculates the true cost of imports (including duties and freight), and enforces quality checks at key points. Reorder rules trigger purchase orders automatically; demand forecasting aligns procurement with sales trends; and import compliance ensures you meet customs and trade regulations. For a Singapore SME importing goods or managing seasonal demand, this prevents costly stockouts, reduces excess inventory, and keeps you compliant with trade rules.

Analytics & Intelligence.narrative: This family provides dashboards and reports on inventory turns, order-to-delivery lead times, fill rates, and supplier performance — giving you visibility into what's working and what's not. You can spot slow-moving stock, identify bottlenecks in your supply chain, and measure supplier reliability. For a Singapore SME, this transforms raw data into actionable insights: you can make faster decisions on what to stock, which suppliers to trust, and where to invest in efficiency.

Consignment Inventory.narrative: This family tracks inventory owned by a supplier but physically held at your warehouse, or your stock held at a customer site — useful for high-value or slow-moving items where you want to defer payment until sale. For a Singapore SME, this is a nice-to-have if you have strategic partnerships with suppliers or customers willing to use consignment terms, but it is not essential for typical operations.

Vendor Managed Inventory.narrative: This family lets a supplier monitor your stock levels and automatically replenish without you raising a purchase order — shifting inventory responsibility to the vendor. For a Singapore SME, this is optional and typically only valuable if you have a large, mature relationship with a supplier who is willing to take on this role and has the systems to support it.

Procurement Analytics & Spend Analysis.narrative: This family provides detailed reporting on procurement spend by vendor, category, and time period, plus metrics like PO cycle time and vendor performance trends. For a Singapore SME, this is useful for strategic sourcing and vendor negotiations, but not essential unless you have complex multi-vendor procurement or significant spend to optimise.

AI & Machine Learning for Supply Chain.narrative: This family applies advanced AI to detect anomalies in demand, predict supply disruptions, and optimise inventory levels automatically using external signals like weather or market trends. For a Singapore SME, this is a future-state capability — valuable only if you have mature data, complex supply chains, and the budget to invest in advanced analytics.
